ps140: Guiding souls by your discourse,* you showed yourself to be a godly steward and a faithful exponent of the divine mysteries, O God-bearer,* sowing the seeds of salvation and reaping fruit in great abundance.* And standing now before your Master, O blessed one,* rejoicing you present it to Him.* Be mindful of this your flock, which ever honours you,* O you who are inspired of God.

Doing battle zealously for Orthodoxy, you were shown to be meek and simple;* for, arrayed in faith as with a breastplate,* and with abstinence as your spear, O divinely wise Nicetas,* you denounced every blasphemous heresy,* honouring and worshipping before the divine image of the Saviour,* and manifestly following the rules of the Fathers, O God-bearer.

When the tyrant most dark with bestial savagery* imprisoned you in bitter exiles and most dark places,* you endured it, O father, bearing in mind the habitation of paradise,* the splendour whereof you have now truly been vouchsafed to behold, O blessed one, *receiving recompense for your pangs.
theotokion: In your womb, O all-holy Bride of God,* you alone bore without confining Him the uncontainable God* who, in His goodness, became man.* Therefore, I beseech you: rescue me from the passions which restrict me,* that, having trod the straight and narrow path,* I may attain to that which leads to life, O Virgin.
stavrotheotokion: When the unblemished ewe-lamb beheld her Lamb* led forth as a man to the slaughter of His own will,* she said, weeping: You now make haste to leave me* who gave You birth childless, O Christ!* What is this which You have done, O Deliverer of all?* Yet, I nevertheless hymn and glorify Your extreme goodness* which passes understanding and recounting, O Lover of mankind!
